skill.name: algorithmic-art
skill.description: Creating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. Use this when users request creating art using code, generative art, algorithmic art, flow fields, or particle systems. Create original algorithmic art rather than copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.
skill.summary: Creating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. U...
skill.author: ArtemisAI

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed in context. The high and critical alerts for weak cryptography, command execution, Python exec, Windows SAM access, and the combined malicious heuristic are false positives from license text, prose, comments, and JavaScript regex parsing. The real risks are legitimate for this skill: it creates executable browser JavaScript artifacts and loads p5.js plus fonts from external CDNs.

medium_findings.title: Executable Browser Artifact Generation
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally produces self-contained HTML artifacts with inline p5.js code and UI handlers. This is expected for generative art, but generated JavaScript can execute in the user's browser context and should be reviewed before sharing or running with untrusted modifications.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly instruct inline browser JavaScript and define UI handlers. This is a legitimate feature, but it creates a real script execution surface.

low_findings.title: External CDN and Font Dependencies
low_findings.description: The viewer template loads p5.js from cdnjs and fonts from Google-hosted domains. This is normal for browser art templates, but it creates network dependency and third-party script trust concerns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The HTML template directly references external URLs for p5.js and fonts. The behavior is clear and expected, with no evidence of data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The critical and high static alerts were dismissed after context review. Apache license text caused weak-cryptography and URL matches, SKILL.md examples are documentation rather than shell execution, generator_template.js uses RegExp.exec for hex parsing, and viewer.html contains a particle system comment rather than Windows SAM access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation, comments, and a regular expression method call rather than credential access, shell execution, or malicious obfuscation. No prompt injection attempt was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Script Load in Template
dangerous_patterns.description: The generated viewer template imports p5.js from an external CDN. This pattern is not malicious here, but remote script loading can execute third-party code if the CDN asset or URL is compromised.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script tag is explicit and browser-executable. The risk is supply-chain exposure, not evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Generative art projects often need a clear concept, reproducible randomness, and interactive controls. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create p5.js artwork from an algorithmic philosophy.
actual_capabilities: Creates an algorithmic philosophy before implementation.
Builds self-contained p5.js HTML art artifacts.
Uses seeded randomness for reproducible visual variations.
Adds sidebar controls for seeds, parameters, reset, and export.
Supports flow fields, particles, recursive systems, and procedural structures.
Provides reusable templates for viewer layout and generator structure.
limitations: Generated HTML loads p5.js and fonts from external CDNs.
Visual quality depends on the model's generated algorithm.
Large particle systems can be slow on low-powered devices.
It does not create server-side rendering pipelines or native applications.

use_cases.title: Prototype Generative Visuals
use_cases.description: Create a browser-based p5.js sketch with repeatable seeds and adjustable parameters.

use_cases.title: Design Interactive Art Studies
use_cases.description: Turn a visual theme into a computational philosophy and an explorable artifact.

use_cases.title: Teach Procedural Art Concepts
use_cases.description: Generate examples that show flow fields, particles, noise, and parameter tuning.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Seeded Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete interactive sketch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an interactive p5.js algorithmic artwork about quiet ocean currents. Include a philosophy, seeded randomness, and simple controls.

prompt_templates.title: Explore Variations
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants several visual options from one concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a generative art piece based on urban rain patterns. Add controls for density, flow strength, palette, and seed navigation.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing Concept
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a theme and wants stronger computational behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Develop an algorithmic philosophy for a forest canopy at dusk, then express it with particles, noise fields, and reproducible variation.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Gallery-Ready Artifact
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for polished interaction and export.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a self-contained p5.js artwork inspired by magnetic interference. Use a refined parameter system, seed presets, and PNG download.

output_examples.input: Create generative art based on desert wind.
output_examples.output: A concise philosophy named Dune Harmonics, followed by an interactive p5.js artifact with seed controls, wind strength, grain density, palette tuning, and PNG export.

output_examples.input: Make a flow-field artwork with reproducible results.
output_examples.output: A browser-ready artwork that uses seeded noise, particle trails, adjustable turbulence, reset controls, and next or previous seed navigation.

output_examples.input: Create an abstract piece about memory and erosion.
output_examples.output: A conceptual movement statement and an interactive sketch where traces fade, rebuild, and vary predictably across user-selected seeds.

best_practices: Review generated JavaScript before running artifacts from untrusted prompts.
Keep parameter ranges small enough for smooth browser performance.
Use fixed seeds when sharing or comparing visual outcomes.
anti_patterns: Do not copy the template without replacing the example algorithm.
Do not add external services beyond the required p5.js and font dependencies.
Do not use random colors or parameters without tying them to the philosophy.

faq.question: Does this skill create finished images?
faq.answer: It creates interactive p5.js artifacts that can be viewed in a browser and exported as PNG images.

faq.question: Can the same artwork be reproduced later?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill emphasizes seeded randomness so the same seed produces the same visual result.

faq.question: Does it require a local server?
faq.answer: No. The output is intended to be a self-contained HTML file that opens directly in a browser.

faq.question: What external resources does it use?
faq.answer: The template loads p5.js from cdnjs and fonts from Google-hosted domains.

faq.question: Can it make animated art?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports static or animated p5.js sketches, depending on the requested concept.

faq.question: Is the generated code safe to run?
faq.answer: The template is not malicious, but generated JavaScript should be reviewed before running or sharing.
